forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over Tasmania will continue to move southeastwards to be centred southeast of Tasmania by Sunday allowing a trough over WA to link to a weak front south of the Bight to move over western and southern parts of SA later Sunday into Monday. Another high pressure system is expected to follow early next week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning easterly in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm inshore.
East to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northeasterly before dawn then decreasing to below 10 knots in the late evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm from the late morning.
North to northwesterly about 10 knots tending northwest to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the morning then tending south to southwesterly in the middle of the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly around 1 metre.
Southerly around 1 metre.
Mostly sunny.
Southeasterly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
